title: Do you think that the writer passed his driving test? Why?

meanings.usage: 通常指官方颁发的许可证书，如驾驶执照 driving licence
指道路上的车辆和行人流动，heavy traffic 表示交通拥挤
指对自己能力的信任，acquire confidence 表示获得信心
指进行考试或检查的人员
指某人做事或完成任务的方式
指给予明确指示或指导，instructed 为过去式/过去分词
用于提出假设情况，let us suppose 表示让我们假设
指对某事物做出反应或回应
作名词指刹车装置，作动词指刹车动作
指用脚操作的装置，如刹车踏板 brake pedal
形容声音或表情带有深深的悲伤
过去式 threw，过去分词 thrown，throw forward 表示向前抛出
指用手或物体对另一物体施加压力，如按刹车
指在中断后继续做某事或不停顿地做下去
指通过努力或过程逐渐获得知识、技能或信心

phrases.usage: 被要求做某事，表示被动地接受指示或要求

examples.en: I had been asked to drive in heavy traffic.
examples.zh: 我被要求在拥挤的交通中驾驶。

phrases.usage: 开车出城，表示从市区驶出

examples.en: After having been instructed to drive out of town, I began to acquire confidence.
examples.zh: 在接到开车出城的指示后，我开始有了信心。

phrases.usage: 确信，确信某事会发生或已经发生

examples.en: Sure that I had passed, I was almost beginning to enjoy my test.
examples.zh: 确信自己已经通过了，我几乎开始享受这次考试。

phrases.usage: 表示对过去发生的事情的肯定推测

examples.en: The examiner must have been pleased with my performance.
examples.zh: 主考官一定对我的表现很满意。

phrases.usage: 一...就...，表示两个动作紧接着发生

examples.en: As soon as I tap on the window, you must stop within five feet.
examples.zh: 我一敲车窗，你就必须在五英尺内停车。

phrases.usage: 花费一些时间，表示某事需要一定的时间才能完成

examples.en: It took me a long time to react.
examples.zh: 我花了很长时间才反应过来。

phrases.usage: 悲伤地看着某人，表示带着悲伤的表情注视

examples.en: The examiner looked at me sadly.

phrases.usage: 用悲伤的声音，表示以哀伤的语调说话

examples.en: He said in a mournful voice.

phrases.usage: 踩刹车踏板，指驾驶员刹车时的动作

examples.en: I suddenly pressed the brake pedal hard.
examples.zh: 我突然用力踩下刹车踏板。

phrases.usage: 被向前抛出，描述因惯性身体前倾的状态

grammar.title: 过去进行时的被动语态 (Past Continuous Tense - Passive Voice)
grammar.definition: 表示过去某一时刻正在进行的被动动作，由 was/were + being + 过去分词构成
grammar.structure: 主语 + was/were + being + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于描述过去某个时刻正在发生的被动动作，强调动作的进行性

examples.en: I was being tested for a driving licence for the third time.
examples.zh: 我正在第三次参加驾照考试。

grammar.title: 过去完成时的被动语态 (Past Perfect Tense - Passive Voice)
grammar.definition: 表示在过去某一时刻之前已经完成的被动动作，由 had + been + 过去分词构成
grammar.structure: 主语 + had + been + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于描述在过去某个时间点之前已经完成的被动动作，常与 after 连用

examples.en: After having been instructed to drive out of town, I began to acquire confidence.
examples.zh: 在被指示开出城后，我开始有了信心。

grammar.title: 情态动词 must 表示对过去的肯定推测 (Must have + 过去分词)
grammar.definition: must have + 过去分词表示对过去发生的事情的肯定推测，意为"一定/肯定已经..."
grammar.structure: 主语 + must have + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于根据现有证据或情况对过去的事情做出强有力的推断，语气比 may/might have 更肯定

examples.en: The examiner must have been pleased with my performance.
examples.zh: 主考官一定对我的表现很满意。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + was/were being + 过去分词

original.en: I was being tested for a driving licence for the third time.
original.zh: 我正在第三次参加驾照考试。

imitations.en: The car was being repaired when I arrived.
imitations.zh: 当我到达时，那辆车正在被修理。

imitations.en: The patient was being examined by the doctor.

sentencePatterns.pattern: After having been + 过去分词...

original.en: After having been instructed to drive out of town, I began to acquire confidence.
original.zh: 在被指示开车出城后，我开始有了信心。

imitations.en: After having been given instructions, we started the project.
imitations.zh: 在接到指示后，我们开始了项目。

imitations.en: After having been told the truth, she felt relieved.
imitations.zh: 在被告知真相后，她感到如释重负。

sentencePatterns.pattern: 主语 + must have been + 过去分词

original.en: The examiner must have been pleased with my performance.
original.zh: 主考官一定对我的表现很满意。

imitations.en: She must have been waiting for hours.
imitations.zh: 她一定已经等了好几个小时。

imitations.en: He must have been sleeping when you called.
imitations.zh: 你打电话时他一定正在睡觉。

sentencePatterns.pattern: As soon as + 主语 + 动词...

original.en: As soon as I tap on the window, you must stop within five feet.
original.zh: 我一敲车窗，你就必须在五英尺内停车。

imitations.en: As soon as the light turns green, you can go.
imitations.zh: 一绿灯亮起，你就可以走了。

imitations.en: As soon as he saw me, he smiled.
